"Back up all selected" is now a robust, server-side batch — and BombVault won't stop itself anymore. From forum testing of a "select all" backup. Thank you.

## Fixed
- **A manual backup no longer dies when the browser does.** Each backup was driven by the HTTP request that started it, so closing the tab, losing the connection, or stopping the container the BombVault UI runs in (e.g. a Chromium container) cancelled the running backup mid-stream. Backups now run under a detached context and survive the client going away.
- **BombVault no longer backs up — and stops — itself.** "Select all" included BombVault's own container; when a backup reached it, BombVault stopped its own container (stop → backup → start), killing the process and the whole run. Its own container is now detected and refused for backup (its settings are recovered separately via **Discover**), excluded from "select all", and shown with a short note instead of a backup button.

## Added
- **Server-side batch backup.** "Back up selected" now kicks off a single request that backs up every selected container **on the server**, one after another, fully independent of your browser — close the tab and it keeps running. Progress streams over SSE: each container shows its own bar, with an overall batch indicator on the Containers page (it reconnects if you reopen the tab). Only one batch runs at a time. New env `BOMBVAULT_SELF_CONTAINER` (set in the template) pins self-detection for host-network setups.

Image: `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/bombvault:2.7.0` (also `:2`, `:2.7`, `:latest`) and `docker.io/junkerderprovinz/bombvault:2.7.0`, multi-arch (amd64 + arm64).
